chunk_summary: This section discusses case-based adjustments to feeding plans for preterm, LBW, and ELBW infants after NICU discharge, emphasizing breastfeeding, breast milk fortification, and tailored nutrition strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of breastfeeding in post-discharge nutrition for preterm and LBW infants.
Learn how to implement breast milk fortification guidelines after NICU discharge.
Identify key considerations for adjusting feeding plans based on individual infant needs.
Review evidence-based protocols for transitioning preterm infants to home feeding.
key_facts: Breastfeeding is recommended as the primary nutrition source for preterm infants post-discharge, with fortification as needed.
Breast milk fortifiers are often used to meet the higher nutritional needs of preterm infants, as per the East of England Neonatal ODN guidelines (2023).
The American Academy of Pediatrics emphasizes tailored nutrition plans for VLBW infants after NICU discharge.
Transitioning from NICU to home requires careful monitoring of growth and nutritional intake.
Clinical Protocol #12 from the Academy of Breastfeeding Medicine provides detailed guidance on transitioning preterm infants to home feeding.
reference_quote: Breast milk fortifier post-discharge guideline (East of England Neonatal ODN, 2023) provides updated recommendations for nutritional supplementation in preterm infants after NICU discharge.
document_key_topics: Breastfeeding and fortification for preterm infants
